name: Sauerkraut
recipeInstructions:
1. For the best sauerkraut, use firm heads of fresh cabbage.
2. Shred cabbage and start kraut fermentation between 24 and 48 hours after harvest.
3. Yield: About 9 quarts.
4. Work with about 5 pounds of cabbage at a time.
5. Discard outer leaves.
6. Rinse heads under cold running water and drain.
7. Cut heads into quarters and remove cores.
8. Shred or slice to a thickness of a quarter.
9. Put shredded cabbage in a suitable fermentation container.
10. Add 3 tablespoons of salt and mix thoroughly with clean hands.
11. Pack the cabbage firmly until the salt draws juices from it.
12. Repeat shredding, salting, and packing until all cabbage is in the container.
13. Ensure the container's rim is at least 4 or 5 inches above the cabbage.
14. If juice does not cover the cabbage, add boiled and cooled brine (1-½ tablespoons of salt per quart of water).
15. Add a plate and weights; cover with a clean bath towel.
16. Store at 70 to 75 degrees Fahrenheit while fermenting.
17. At temperatures between 70 and 75 degrees Fahrenheit, kraut will be fully fermented in about 3 to 4 weeks.
18. At temperatures between 60 and 65 degrees Fahrenheit, fermentation may take 5 to 6 weeks.
19. Below 60 degrees Fahrenheit, kraut may not ferment.
20. Above 75 degrees Fahrenheit, kraut may become soft.
21. If using a brine-filled bag to weigh the cabbage down, do not disturb the crock until normal fermentation is completed (when bubbling ceases).
22. If using jars as weights, check the kraut 2 to 3 times each week and remove scum if it forms.
23. Fully fermented kraut may be kept tightly covered in the refrigerator for several months or canned:
- Hot pack: Bring kraut and liquid to a boil in a large kettle, stirring frequently. Remove from heat and fill jars firmly with kraut and juices, leaving ½-inch headspace.
- Raw pack: Fill jars firmly with kraut and cover with juices, leaving ½-inch headspace.
24. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1:
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Sauerkraut in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Pints
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 min
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 15 min
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 15 min
Above 6,000 ft: 20 min
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Quarts
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 20 min
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 25 min
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 30 min
Above 6,000 ft: 35 min
Style of Pack: Raw
Jar Size: Pints
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 20 min
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 25 min
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 30 min
Above 6,000 ft: 35 min
Style of Pack: Raw
Jar Size: Quarts
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 25 min
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 30 min
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 35 min
Above 6,000 ft: 40 min.
